---
title: "Correlation Analysis - Environmental Variables"
author: "Rita Verstraeten"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

# Correlation Analysis of Environmental Covariates

## Setup

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
source("renv/activate.R")
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
# install.packages(c("lubridate", "readr"))
library(lubridate)
library(readr)
library(DT)
library(ggplot2)
library(readxl)
library(tidyr)  # for pivot_longer
library(corrplot)
library(rstatix)


# if (interactive() && isTRUE(capabilities("httpgd"))) {
#   httpgd::hgd(silent = TRUE)  # use httpgd only in interactive sessions
# }
library(httpgd)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 6)


# Load your processed data
env_all <- read_csv("/media/rita/New Volume/Documenten/DI-MOB/Other Data/Env_data_cuba/data/all_environmental_data_per_manzana_2016_04_to_2019_12.csv")
```

## 1. Correlation between numeric variables

### Select the variables
```{r select_vars}
categorical_vars <- c( "is_WI","is_WUI","is_urban","landcover")
numeric_vars <- c("mean_ndvi", "mean_ndwi", "mean_ndmi", 
                  "avg_temp", "total_precip", "rel_hum", "WS2M", "WS10M", "WS50M")

# Create subset with only numeric env variables
env_num <- env_all %>%
  select(all_of(numeric_vars)) %>%
  drop_na()

head(env_num)
dim(env_num)
```

### Compute Correlation Matrix

```{r corr_matrix}
# Pearson correlation
corr_matrix <- cor(env_num, method = "pearson")

# View the matrix
round(corr_matrix, 3)
```

### Visualize Correlations

```{r corr_plot}
png(
  filename = "/home/rita/PyProjects/DI-MOB-BionamiX/results/Entomo/images/correlation_matrix_numeric_vars.png",
  width = 10,
  height = 10,
  units = "in",
  res = 300,
  bg = "white"
)
# Correlation heatmap
corrplot(corr_matrix, 
         method = "color",
         type = "upper",
         addCoef.col = "black",
         diag = FALSE,
         tl.col = "black")
dev.off()

```

## 2. Correlation between categorical variables 
### Select the variables
```{r correlation categorical vars}

# Select all categorical variables
categorical_data <- env_all %>%
  select(all_of(categorical_vars)) %>%
  drop_na()

head(categorical_data)
```

### Calculate the correlation 
```{r calculate the cat var Cramers_v}
# Create Cramér's V matrix manually
cat_vars_list <- colnames(categorical_data)
n_vars <- length(cat_vars_list)
cramers_v <- matrix(NA, nrow = n_vars, ncol = n_vars)
rownames(cramers_v) <- cat_vars_list
colnames(cramers_v) <- cat_vars_list

for (i in 1:n_vars) {
  for (j in 1:n_vars) {
    if (i == j) {
      cramers_v[i, j] <- 1.0
    } else if (i < j) {
      # Chi-square test
      contingency_table <- table(categorical_data[[i]], categorical_data[[j]])
      chi2 <- chisq.test(contingency_table)$statistic
      n <- sum(contingency_table)
      min_dim <- min(nrow(contingency_table), ncol(contingency_table))
      cramers_v[i, j] <- sqrt(chi2 / (n * (min_dim - 1)))
      cramers_v[j, i] <- cramers_v[i, j]
    }
  }
}

round(cramers_v, 3)
```
### Visualize

```{r visualize correlation cat var}
png(
  filename = "/home/rita/PyProjects/DI-MOB-BionamiX/results/Entomo/images/correlation_matrix_categorical_cramers_v.png",
  width = 8,
  height = 8,
  units = "in",
  res = 300,
  bg = "white"
)

corrplot(cramers_v, 
         method = "color",
         type = "upper",
         addCoef.col = "black",
         diag = FALSE,
         tl.col = "black")

dev.off()
```


## 5. Mixed Correlation (Numeric + Binary)

```{r mixed_corr}
# Point-biserial correlation (numeric vs binary)
# Use Spearman or Kendall which work better for mixed types

mixed_data <- env_all %>%
  select(all_of(numeric_vars), starts_with("is_")) %>%
  mutate(across(starts_with("is_"), as.numeric)) %>%
  drop_na()

# Spearman (rank-based, more robust)
spearman_corr <- cor(mixed_data, method = "spearman")

png(
  filename = "/home/rita/PyProjects/DI-MOB-BionamiX/results/Entomo/images/correlation_matrix_mixed_spearman.png",
  width = 12,
  height = 12,
  units = "in",
  res = 300,
  bg = "white"
)

corrplot(spearman_corr, 
         method = "color",
         type = "upper",
         addCoef.col = "black",
         diag = FALSE,
         tl.col = "black",
         tl.cex = 0.8)

dev.off()
```
